locations.name: Hyatt Regency San Antonio Riverwalk
locations.note: Home base for the weekend. Directly on the Riverwalk, steps from everything downtown.

locations.name: Alamodome
locations.note: Main event — Ye concert, Saturday July 4 at 9 PM. Tickets in hand. Major sports and concert venue about a mile southeast of the Riverwalk.

locations.name: Frost Bank Center
locations.note: Home of the San Antonio Spurs (NBA). Also hosts major concerts and the annual San Antonio Stock Show & Rodeo. East of downtown, about 2 miles from the hotel.

locations.name: Toyota Field
locations.note: Home of San Antonio FC (USL Championship soccer). Smaller stadium on the north side, about 10 miles from downtown.

locations.name: Nelson W. Wolff Municipal Stadium
locations.note: Home of the San Antonio Missions (MiLB, Padres affiliate). Retro ballpark feel on the west side, about 7 miles from downtown.

locations.name: The Alamo
locations.note: The most iconic landmark in Texas — site of the 1836 battle. Free to enter, right on Alamo Plaza a short walk from the hotel. Worth a look even if history isn't your thing.

locations.name: San Antonio Museum of Art
locations.note: Riverside art museum with a collection spanning 5,000 years. Sits on the Museum Reach section of the River Walk — reachable by water taxi. Free the first Sunday of every month.

locations.name: Hopscotch
locations.note: Immersive digital art experience with a full bar inside. Walkable from the hotel and Riverwalk. Good pre-night-out stop.

locations.name: Witte Museum
locations.note: Natural history and Texas heritage museum in Brackenridge Park. Dinosaurs, nature, and deep Texas history. About 3 miles north of downtown.

locations.name: San Antonio Zoo
locations.note: One of the top-rated zoos in the country, set in Brackenridge Park. About 3 miles north of downtown. Open daily 9am–5pm.

locations.name: Ruby City
locations.note: Contemporary art museum with free admission in Southtown, near the Blue Star arts complex. Rotating exhibitions in a striking jewel-like building designed by David Adjaye.

locations.name: Aztec Theatre
locations.note: Historic 1920s movie palace converted into one of SA's best live-music venues. On the river, walkable from the hotel.

locations.name: Hog Wild Records
locations.note: Long-running SA record shop on Main Ave with a deep catalog of vinyl and CDs. Good browse for diggers.

locations.name: Pinkerton's Barbecue
locations.note: Acclaimed Texas BBQ on Houston St, walkable from the hotel. Expect a line — go early or right at opening.

locations.name: Curry Boys BBQ
locations.note: Thai-BBQ fusion near St. Mary's Strip, highly rated. A mashup of Thai flavors and smoked meats that works better than it sounds.

locations.name: 2M Smokehouse
locations.note: Beloved South Texas BBQ on the southeast side. Thu–Sun only, lunch hours, frequently sells out — go early.

locations.name: DeeWillie's BBQ
locations.note: BBQ and live music on the northeast side. Laid-back afternoon smoke session with a neighborhood feel.

locations.name: Los Trompos Tacos de Barrio
locations.note: Trompo/al pastor tacos on the west side, dinner hours. Classic SA street-style tacos done right.

locations.name: Taquitos West Avenue
locations.note: Classic old-school taqueria open until 3 AM Fri/Sat with honor-system ordering. The go-to late-night taco stop.

locations.name: Con Huevos Tacos
locations.note: Breakfast and brunch tacos just east of downtown on Houston St. Morning only — closes at 2 PM.

locations.name: Q Kitchen Bar
locations.note: The Hyatt's own restaurant — American diner-style, open breakfast through late, with live music. Literally steps from the room.

locations.name: Boudro's on the Riverwalk
locations.note: Riverwalk classic with Tex-Mex, tableside guac, and prickly pear margaritas. 4.5 stars — reservations recommended.

locations.name: Rita's on the River
locations.note: Tex-Mex right on the river with a festive patio and big margaritas. 4.6 stars, closed Sundays.

locations.name: Domingo Restaurant
locations.note: Modern Mexican in the Canopy hotel, strong for breakfast, brunch, and tacos. 4.7 stars.

locations.name: Biga on the Banks
locations.note: Upscale New American fine dining on the river, dinner only. The special-occasion spot of the trip — 4.7 stars.

locations.name: Stout's Signature
locations.note: Steak and cocktail bar near the river, dinner only, closed Sundays. 4.9 stars — highest-rated restaurant on the map.

locations.name: Four Brothers
locations.note: New American with riverfront tables, all-day hours, and live guitar. Relaxed vibe on the museum reach of the river.

locations.name: Pinoy Tambayan
locations.note: Filipino street food on the west side, open until 4 AM with karaoke. The late-night option when everything else is closed.

locations.name: The Golden Goose
locations.note: Korean/Japanese spot downtown with kiosk ordering and late hours. Good cheap eat after a night out.

locations.name: Pelicana Chicken
locations.note: Korean fried chicken on the north side (De Zavala). A solid late-night wing run.

locations.name: Kaedama Battleship
locations.note: Ramen and Japanese comfort food on Houston St, walkable from the hotel. Good pre-bar or late-night bowl.

locations.name: La Panadería
locations.note: Mexican bakery café on Broadway with exceptional pastries and breakfast. Good morning stop on the way to the zoo or Witte.

locations.name: Chas Market and Kitchen
locations.note: Korean bodega and kitchen near the Pearl/Government Hill. Good for snacks, drinks, or a quick meal.

locations.name: Golden Wok
locations.note: Pan-Asian and dim sum in the Medical Center area on the northwest side. One of the better dim sum spots in SA.

locations.name: Neko Coffee & Plants
locations.note: Japanese coffee shop and plant store on Callaghan Rd. Distinctive vibe, good matcha and pour-overs.

locations.name: Hugman's Oasis
locations.note: Downtown tiki bar with immersive theming and craft cocktails, walkable from the hotel. One of the more distinctive bar concepts in SA.

locations.name: Tenfold Rooftop
locations.note: 4.9-star rooftop cocktail bar near the hotel on S. Alamo. Great views — strong pregame option.

locations.name: Anaqua Garden Bar
locations.note: Lush garden and pool bar on S. Alamo with craft cocktails. 4.9 stars, one of the best-rated bars in the area.

locations.name: The Moon's Daughters
locations.note: 20th-floor rooftop bar atop the Thompson hotel with city views and a DJ on weekends. Strong for a late-night cap.

locations.name: Otro Bar
locations.note: Stylish riverside cocktail bar (4.9 stars) sharing the Canopy/Domingo space. Open late Fri/Sat.

locations.name: Howl at the Moon
locations.note: Dueling-pianos request bar on the Riverwalk with rowdy group energy. Perfect for a loud, fun night out.

locations.name: Wav Room
locations.note: Late-night dance club on the St. Mary's Strip. One of the main spots on the strip for dancing.

locations.name: Volta
locations.note: Intimate vinyl and cocktail bar with a retro vibe near the Pearl. Good for a quieter drink with good music.

locations.name: Maeve
locations.note: Open-air DJ and event bar near downtown with a cover charge on weekends. Known for a younger crowd and good production.

locations.name: The Lonesome Rose
locations.note: Honky-tonk dive on the St. Mary's Strip with live music and cheap drinks. No frills, good energy.

locations.name: Evil Olive
locations.note: Bar and grill on the north side (Thousand Oaks) with live music and karaoke. Drive-to destination — not walkable.

locations.name: Coco Beach
locations.note: Laid-back dive bar and club on the north side with a pool table. Very casual, cheap drinks.

locations.name: Charlie Brown's Neighborhood Bar & Grill
locations.note: Sports bar on the north side with darts and pool. Low-key neighborhood vibe.

locations.name: The Hangar Bar & Grill
locations.note: Bar and grill on Broadway with karaoke and trivia nights. Good hang in the Alamo Heights corridor.

locations.name: Schooners
locations.note: Dive sports bar near the NE 410 loop with darts and pool. No-frills neighborhood bar.

locations.name: The Rusty Nail
locations.note: Neighborhood bar and food on the far west side off Potranco Rd. Far from downtown — Uber territory.

locations.name: River Walk / Downtown
locations.note: Central hub: bars, dining, the main artery.

locations.name: Alamo Plaza
locations.note: The Alamo + historic tourist cluster.

locations.name: Houston St / E. Commerce
locations.note: Downtown bar and club stretch.

locations.name: St. Mary's Strip
locations.note: The city's main bar / live-music strip.

locations.name: The Pearl
locations.note: Upscale food hall, brewery, trendy bars.

locations.name: Southtown / Blue Star
locations.note: Arts district, craft cocktails, breweries.

locations.name: Historic Market Square
locations.note: El Mercado, Tex-Mex, shopping.

locations.name: Broadway / Alamo Heights
locations.note: Broadway corridor: bars, restaurants, museums, the zoo.

locations.name: Thousand Oaks / North Side
locations.note: North-side neighborhood bars & grills.
